title: Font Pairing Spec — Outfit vs Sora
body:
# Font Pairing Spec — Outfit vs Sora

## Recommendation: **Outfit** for headings

### Why Outfit over Sora
| Criterion | Outfit | Sora |
|-----------|--------|------|
| Personality | Rounded, friendly, modern — matches personality/wellbeing brand tone | Geometric, more neutral/corporate |
| Weight range | 100–900 (full range, great for contrast) | 100–800 |
| Legibility at large sizes | Excellent — distinctive letterforms at 48px+ | Good but more generic |
| Legibility at small sizes | Very good — no overcrowding | Good |
| Google Fonts availability | Yes — single `@import` | Yes |
| Brand fit: personality platform | ✅ Warm, curious, approachable | Neutral — neither helps nor hurts |

**Decision: Outfit.** It has the right warmth for a personality intelligence product without being playful/childish. Sora reads more B2B SaaS.

## Font Loading

```html
<!-- In <head> — preconnect first for speed -->
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@400;500;600;700;800&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
```

Load only weights in use: 400 (regular), 600 (semibold), 700 (bold), 800 (extrabold). Skip 100-300 and 900 — not used in spec below.

## Type Scale

| Element | Font | Weight | Size (desktop) | Size (mobile) | Line-height | Letter-spacing |
|---------|------|--------|---------------|--------------|-------------|---------------|
| `h1` (hero headline) | Outfit | 800 | 56px | 36px | 1.1 | -0.02em |
| `h2` (section headline) | Outfit | 700 | 40px | 28px | 1.15 | -0.01em |
| `h3` (card title) | Outfit | 600 | 24px | 20px | 1.2 | 0 |
| `h4` (sub-label) | Outfit | 600 | 18px | 16px | 1.3 | 0 |
| Body (paragraphs) | System | 400 | 16px | 15px | 1.6 | 0 |
| Caption / label | System | 500 | 13px | 12px | 1.4 | 0.02em |
| Button text | Outfit | 600 | 16px | 15px | 1 | 0.01em |

`system` = `system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif` (already in `--sans` CSS var)

## CSS Variables Update

```css
:root {
  --font-display: Outfit, system-ui, sans-serif;
  --sans: system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif;
}

h1, h2, h3, h4 { font-family: var(--font-display); }
.btn-start      { font-family: var(--font-display); font-weight: 600; }
body, p, li     { font-family: var(--sans); }
```

## Performance Notes
- `display=swap` ensures no invisible text during font load
- Preconnect links eliminate DNS + TLS round-trips
- 5 weights at ~10KB each ≈ 50KB total (acceptable; loads async, swap prevents blocking)
- Body stays on system fonts = **zero additional network requests** for body text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Outfit loaded via Google Fonts with preconnect, `display=swap`
- [ ] All h1–h4 and buttons use Outfit via `--font-display` CSS var
- [ ] Body/paragraph text remains on system fonts
- [ ] Hero h1 renders at 56px/800 weight on desktop, 36px on mobile
- [ ] No layout shift (CLS) from font swap — verify in Lighthouse
